title: 3. Startup Self-Check
description: A guided checklist for the pieces that must be ready before the companion can start: Python install result, LM Studio, FFmpeg, model selection, and console errors.

steps.title: What Startup Needs
steps.body: Neural Companion starts cleanly when the installer finished, LM Studio is serving a model, FFmpeg is available, and the selected TTS/avatar runtimes match what is installed.

steps.title: Check the Console First
steps.body: The console is the first place to look after a failed start. Missing Python packages, unavailable models, Hugging Face access, and avatar-pack paths usually show up here.

steps.title: Refresh Models
steps.body: If LM Studio is running but no model appears, refresh the model list. The model selector should show the model you intend to use before initialization.

steps.title: Use a Light Baseline
steps.body: For troubleshooting, use Avatar Engine None or MuseTalk with Very Low VRAM. Avoid changing several heavy settings at once until the app can start repeatedly.

steps.title: Stop Before Retrying
steps.body: If startup partly succeeded, terminate the session before trying a different configuration. A clean restart avoids mixing old worker state with new settings.

steps.title: Healthy Startup
steps.body: A healthy startup means the model is selected, initialization finishes without a traceback, and chat/TTS can complete a short test reply. After that, move on to presets and avatars.
